title: centaurea karabaghensis
intro: Centaurea karabaghensis, also known as Karabagh cornflower, is a perennial plant native to the Caucasus region. It belongs to the daisy family and is recognized for its bright blue flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are believed to aid in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Centaurea karaghensis can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves can also be consumed raw in salads, providing potential health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
